title: Ainsdale Dunes Eco-Adventure Hub
description: An all-in-one electric fat-bike and sand-board rental kiosk catering to Ainsdale's growing eco-tourism. Offers guided sand-dune trail packages and digital trail guides via QR codes, tapping into local interest in Southport's natural coastline.
evidence: Recent consultations on Sefton coastal plan and the surge in outdoor staycations. Ainsdale has no active bike rentals directly on the beach trail entrance, despite over 100,000 annual visitors.

title: Birkdale Artisanal Prep-Kitchen & Pantry
description: A collaborative, shared commercial kitchen facility designed for local home-bakers and street-food sellers in Birkdale. Solves the Sefton Council compliance hurdle for micro-businesses looking to scale without heavy commercial leases.
evidence: Closure of several small cafes in Birkdale leaving a gap for locally prepared craft bakes, combined with a 35% rise in home-based food registries in Sefton since 2024.

title: Churchtown Cargo-Bike Local Express
description: An eco-friendly, cargo-bike delivery collective serving Churchtown and Southport town centre's boutique retailers. Enables local florists, bakers, and makers to offer competitive same-day home delivery to PR8/PR9 residents.
evidence: Lord Street retail transformation strategy focusing on local independents rather than big chains. Retailers have expressed high delivery costs via traditional parcel couriers.
